originalTitle: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this […]
content: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley’s Hound Dog to Beyoncé’s Say My Name, and Harry Styles’ As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this list represents “only a small portion of Plaintiffs’ works that Udio infringed,” as reported earlier by Music Business Worldwide. In 2024, Sony, along with Universal Music Group and Warner Records, filed a lawsuit against Udio and the AI music-maker Suno. After gaining access to Udio’s training data through discovery, Sony claims it identified additional songs using an “audio fingerprinting” technique. Sony filed a request to add more than 30,000 songs that Udio allegedly “copied and ingested into its generative AI models,” but a judge later rejected the company’s motion, leaving the scope of the original lawsuit at 333 works. Sony has now made the more than 30,000 songs the subject of its new lawsuit, which the company says it could expand. It claims Udio “admitted that its generative AI models were ‘constructed by showing the program a vast amount of different kinds of sound recordings,’” including from YouTube. UMG and Warner Music Group have since settled with Udio and are now partnering with the company as the music industry embraces AI. You can read the full list of songs Sony is suing Udio over in this filing. The company is asking to block Udio from infringing its works, as well as for up to $150,000 per work infringed.
chineseTitle: 以下是索尼起诉 Udio AI 音乐生成器的 30,000 首歌曲
chineseSummary: 索尼音乐娱乐公司又对 Udio 提起诉讼，指控人工智能音乐生成器侵犯了其 30,000 多首歌曲的版权，其中包括埃尔维斯·普雷斯利 (Elvis Presley) 的《Hound Dog》、碧昂丝 (Beyoncé) 的《Say My Name》和哈利·斯泰尔斯 (Harry Styles) 的《As It Was》。该诉讼于周一在纽约法院提起，声称这 [...]
chineseContent: 索尼音乐娱乐公司又对 Udio 提起诉讼，指控人工智能音乐生成器侵犯了其 30,000 多首歌曲的版权，其中包括埃尔维斯·普雷斯利 (Elvis Presley) 的《Hound Dog》、碧昂斯 (Beyoncé) 的《Say My Name》和哈利·斯泰尔斯 (Harry Styles) 的《As It Was》。据《音乐商业全球》早些时候报道，周一向纽约法院提起的诉讼声称，这份清单“仅代表 Udio 侵权的原告作品的一小部分”。 2024 年，索尼与环球音乐集团和华纳唱片公司一起对 Udio 和人工智能音乐制作商 Suno 提起诉讼。在通过发现获得 Udio 的训练数据后，索尼声称它使用“音频指纹”技术识别了更多歌曲。索尼提出了添加 30,000 多首歌曲的请求，据称这些歌曲是 Udio“复制并摄取到其生成人工智能模型中”的，但法官后来驳回了该公司的动议，将最初诉讼的范围保留在 333 首作品。索尼现已将这 30,000 多首歌曲作为其新诉讼的主题，该公司表示可能会扩大诉讼范围。据称，Udio“承认其生成式人工智能模型是‘通过向程序展示大量不同类型的录音而构建的’”，其中包括来自 YouTube 的录音。 UMG 和华纳音乐集团已与 Udio 达成和解，并随着音乐行业拥抱人工智能而与该公司合作。您可以在这份文件中阅读索尼起诉 Udio 的完整歌曲列表。该公司要求阻止 Udio 侵犯其作品，并为每件侵权作品支付最高 15 万美元的赔偿金。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
